markdownRemark.html:
Introduction

Component data caching has obvious advantages in improving performance, reducing server load, lowering network traffic, and enhancing user experience. It is a commonly used optimization technique in component development.

In the Choerodon UI component library, data caching is implemented based on DataSet and mainly manifests in the following aspects:

  • Table component:

    • When the associated DataSet has the corresponding cache configuration enabled, data that is selected or changed after switching pages in the Table will be cached in the DataSet instance.
    • Additionally, you can configure how the cached data is displayed by setting props on the Table component.

  • Select component:

    • Data in the dropdown list is cached by default. The cache size and duration can be configured, and you can manually call the corresponding methods to refresh the cached data.

  • Lov component:

    • View configuration data is cached by default and cannot be changed.
    • View display data is also cached by default, but the cache configuration can be changed by setting the corresponding props.

Core Concepts of DataSet Data Caching

The data source DataSet can manage cached data through configuration (props), methods (methods), and values after instantiation (values). When used with related components, the corresponding configurations and methods reflect relevant interaction designs.

Related Values

After enabling the cached data setting, the DataSet will classify and manage data. all contains all related records, including those in records and cachedRecords, while the cachedRecords attribute stores the selected and modified records saved when the cached data is enabled.

Classify data source data according to record.status:

Related Methods (Categorized by Application Scenarios)

Query, Reset, and Verification Related Scenarios

  • query(page, params, cache), by default calling the query does not retain cached data

    • page<optional, default:1> - specify the page number
    • params - temporary query parameters
    • cache (1.5.0-beta.0) - whether to retain changes in the cache

  • loadData(data, total, cache) - By default, cached data is not retained when loading

    • data - data array
    • total - total count, optional, used for pagination
    • cache (1.5.0-beat.0) - whether to retain changes in the cache
  • reset() - resets changes and clears validation status (will not reset cached data in the cachedRecords value property)
  • validate() - checks whether records are valid (will validate cached data)

Scenarios Related to Cache Data Operations

  • Getting Cache: You can directly use the cachedRecords value property to get all cached records in the DataSet instance (including selected and modified records). If you want more detailed data, you can use the cachedSelected (cached selected records) and cachedModified (cached modified records) value properties.

  • Custom Setting Cache:

    • Set Cached Selected Records: Directly assign a value to the cachedSelected value property of the DataSet instance. Note that the record being assigned must have its isSelected and isCached properties set to true.
    • Set Cached Modified Records: Directly assign a value to the cachedModified value property of the DataSet instance.

  • Clearing Cache:

    • clearCachedSelected(): Clears cached selected records
    • clearCachedModified(): Clears cached modified records
    • clearCachedRecords(): Clears all cached records

Submission Scenarios

Submit data through the DataSet or retrieve the JSON data from the DataSet yourself.

Notes

  • toJSONData(): Converts the corresponding records into JSON data for submission based on the dataToJSON property set in the DataSet (including data in cached).
  • toData(): Converts all records in the records and cachedModified value properties into plain data, excluding deleted data.

Besides the above directly cache-related properties and methods, based on actual business scenarios, you can manage cached data more flexibly in combination with other DataSet configurations and methods. For more detailed descriptions of properties, see: DataSet Documentation

Component Practical Application

Table

When initializing a DataSet instance, you can configure whether the selected and changed data (data and record refer to record entries, the same below) are cached. The cached data is stored in the value property of the above DataSet instance.

Caching Scenarios

  1. Cache Disabled Configuration Scenario Interaction

    1. After selecting and modifying data, clicking Next Page prompts that there is unsaved data.
    2. After switching pages, the selected and modified data are not saved.

  1. Cache Enabled Configuration Scenario Interaction

    1. After selecting and modifying data, clicking Next Page does not prompt about unsaved data; it directly goes to the next page.
    2. The selected and modified data can be viewed by clicking the button at the bottom left.

Related Properties

Through the above examples, DataSet can be configured to enable data caching, which is divided into two types: selected (checked) and modified (including newly added, modified, and temporarily deleted data).

  • Configuration Methods

    • cacheRecords property in global configuration (configure)
    • cacheSelection and cacheModified properties in DataSet

Cached Record Tip Configuration

When the DataSet instance associated with a Table has caching enabled (or global configuration has caching enabled), the Table has corresponding properties to control cache tips.

  • Table Related Properties:

    • showSelectionTips: whether to display tips for selected records
  • showCachedTips: whether to display cached record tips, has higher priority than showSelectionTips
  • showCachedSelection: whether to display cached selected records
  • onShowCachedSelectionChange: callback for displaying cached selected records
  • showSelectionCachedButton: whether to display the cached selected records button, default is true

  • configure Related Properties:

    • tableShowSelectionTips: default display of selected record tips, default is false
  • tableShowCachedTips: By default, displays cached record tips. It has a higher priority than tableShowSelectionTips, default is false.

Configuration Scene Display

  1. The table uses the default configuration above, before turning the page
  1. Using the default configuration above for the table, after turning the page
  1. Table Adjust the above settings to enable cache-related prompts, after turning the page

Select All Across Pages

  • DataSet Related

    • isAllPageSelection (value property): Indicates whether it is in a cross-page all-selection state. Please use it together with unSelected (value property) to submit cross-page selected data. Interface support is required. Note: After selecting cross-page all-selection, it only marks the all-selection state and does not affect the amount of data in the records value property.
    • setAllPageSelection(enabled) (method): Toggle whether to enable cross-page all-selection

  • Table Related

    • showAllPageSelectionButton (property): Whether to display the button to toggle cross-page all-selection

Select

Basic Introduction

  • The data in the Select dropdown is cached by default. The cache size, duration, and other settings are configured in the global configure lookupCache property.
  • The dropdown data of the Select component can be obtained by calling the field's method, where you can specify whether to return cached data or to query again.

Cache-related Configuration

  • Select Component Properties

    • noCache: Automatically re-query when dropdown opens, does not cache dropdown data source

  • Field Methods

    • fetchLookup(noCache, record) (method): Requests lookup data; returns cached data if available (noCache - whether to disable cache)

  • Global Configuration (configure)

    • lookupCache (property): Lookup cache configuration.

      • maxAge - cache duration
      • max - maximum number of cache items, default value: { maxAge: 1000 * 60 * 10, max: 100 }

Lov

Basic Introduction

  • Lov data is divided into two parts:

    • View configuration data: Query settings for displaying pop-up views, which are cached by default and cannot be changed.
    • View display data: Cached by default, but caching settings can be modified using the globally configured lovNoCache property or the Lov component's noCache property.

  • The optional data display in Lov is essentially the display of a Table:

    • For associated data sources, the cache selected records (cacheSelection) configuration is enabled by default.
    • When switching pages in the pop-up, the selected data is cached.
    • The Table's show cached selected records button is displayed by default (Table's showSelectionCachedButton property).
    • Table-related properties can be modified by setting the tableProps property on the Lov component.

Cache-related Configuration

  • Lov Component Properties

    • noCache: Automatically re-query when the popup appears, default is false

  • Global Configuration (configure)

    • lovNoCache: Default Lov noCache
    • lovQueryCachedSelected: Query cached selected records in Lov. When Lov is multi-select, before opening the popup, it queries the cached selected records in the cache data source to ensure their data is accurate
